texts.label: text
texts.orig: Comprehensive test fixture for the Anti-Gravity DOCX pipeline
texts.text: Comprehensive test fixture for the Anti-Gravity DOCX pipeline

texts.label: text
texts.orig:
This document is designed to test every structural element that the Anti-Gravity pipeline is expected to handle faithfully. The document deliberately includes complex layouts, merged-cell tables, multiple embedded chart images, and a plain photograph to ensure that the chart-heuristic works correctly.

The pipeline converts this document through the following stages:
Docling structural parse → Table extraction → Image OCR (Textract) → Chart semantics (Bedrock Nova Lite) → Markdown + JSON output.
texts.text:
This document is designed to test every structural element that the Anti-Gravity pipeline is expected to handle faithfully. The document deliberately includes complex layouts, merged-cell tables, multiple embedded chart images, and a plain photograph to ensure that the chart-heuristic works correctly.

The pipeline converts this document through the following stages:
Docling structural parse → Table extraction → Image OCR (Textract) → Chart semantics (Bedrock Nova Lite) → Markdown + JSON output.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Reading order across multiple paragraphs is tested here. This paragraph comes immediately after the formatting demo. The pipeline should preserve the logical sequence of all text blocks when converting to Markdown.
texts.text: Reading order across multiple paragraphs is tested here. This paragraph comes immediately after the formatting demo. The pipeline should preserve the logical sequence of all text blocks when converting to Markdown.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A second body paragraph follows. Docling preserves reading order even when the source document has multi-column or text-box layouts by analysing the structural XML rather than visual flow.
texts.text: A second body paragraph follows. Docling preserves reading order even when the source document has multi-column or text-box layouts by analysing the structural XML rather than visual flow.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: OCR via Amazon Textract (managed, no local binary)
texts.text: OCR via Amazon Textract (managed, no local binary)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Chart classification via AWS Bedrock Nova Lite
texts.text: Chart classification via AWS Bedrock Nova Lite

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The table below represents a sample claims summary report.
texts.text: The table below represents a sample claims summary report.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This table tests the pipeline's ability to preserve merged cells in the raw_cells output. The first two cells in row 1 are merged horizontally; the first cell in rows 2-3 is merged vertically.
texts.text: This table tests the pipeline's ability to preserve merged cells in the raw_cells output. The first two cells in row 1 are merged horizontally; the first cell in rows 2-3 is merged vertically.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The bar chart below shows the number of claims filed per quarter. The pipeline should detect this as a chart and call Bedrock for semantic extraction.
texts.text: The bar chart below shows the number of claims filed per quarter. The pipeline should detect this as a chart and call Bedrock for semantic extraction.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This multi-series line chart compares monthly premium collected against claims paid. The Bedrock VLM should identify this as a line chart and extract the two data series.
texts.text: This multi-series line chart compares monthly premium collected against claims paid. The Bedrock VLM should identify this as a line chart and extract the two data series.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The pie chart illustrates portfolio distribution across lines of business. The Bedrock VLM should classify this as a pie chart.
texts.text: The pie chart illustrates portfolio distribution across lines of business. The Bedrock VLM should classify this as a pie chart.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This image simulates a site photograph attached to a property claim. It should NOT trigger the Bedrock VLM call because it does not look like a chart (too few OCR tokens or wrong heuristic profile).
texts.text: This image simulates a site photograph attached to a property claim. It should NOT trigger the Bedrock VLM call because it does not look like a chart (too few OCR tokens or wrong heuristic profile).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This section tests that the pipeline correctly preserves reading order even when the document uses multiple heading levels, nested lists, and sections with varying indentation. The Docling parser operates on the OOXML structure rather than the rendered page, so reading order is determined by element position in the XML tree.
texts.text: This section tests that the pipeline correctly preserves reading order even when the document uses multiple heading levels, nested lists, and sections with varying indentation. The Docling parser operates on the OOXML structure rather than the rendered page, so reading order is determined by element position in the XML tree.

texts.label: text
texts.orig:
This test document covers the full breadth of features supported by the Anti-Gravity pipeline:

• Structural text (headings H1–H3, paragraphs)
• Inline formatting (bold, italic, underline)
• Ordered and unordered lists
• Simple data tables with header rows
• Merged-cell tables (colspan and rowspan)
• Embedded chart images (bar, line, pie)
• Non-chart images (photo)
• Reading-order across multiple sections

After processing, the output directory should contain:
  AntiGravity_Test/AntiGravity_Test.md
  AntiGravity_Test/AntiGravity_Test.dom.json
  AntiGravity_Test/AntiGravity_Test.semantic.json
  AntiGravity_Test/images/picture_0.png  (bar chart)
  AntiGravity_Test/images/picture_1.png  (line chart)
  AntiGravity_Test/images/picture_2.png  (pie chart)
  AntiGravity_Test/images/picture_3.png  (photo — no VLM)
texts.text:
This test document covers the full breadth of features supported by the Anti-Gravity pipeline:

• Structural text (headings H1–H3, paragraphs)

• Ordered and unordered lists
• Simple data tables with header rows
• Merged-cell tables (colspan and rowspan)
• Embedded chart images (bar, line, pie)
• Non-chart images (photo)
• Reading-order across multiple sections

After processing, the output directory should contain:
